jokes.text: Dean struggling to open ribbon with his teeth, saying 'The legitimate!' repeatedly while fumbling
jokes.setup: Formal ribbon-cutting ceremony for new Subway on campus
jokes.punchline: Dean can't even operate scissors properly, undermining the 'legitimate' announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dean's incompetence undermining his own grand announcements. Physical comedy enhances the verbal repetition.

jokes.text: That should be my sandwich shop. It was my idea to put one there.
jokes.setup: Grand opening of corporate Subway displacing local business
jokes.punchline: Shirley's passive-aggressive claim of ownership/credit
jokes.explanation: Establishes Shirley's entrepreneurial jealousy with efficient character work.

jokes.text: They're tenting our building for termites, so we're homeless. I'm staying in the sleep study lab. All I have to do is wake up every three hours and go... And I get two credits.
jokes.punchline: Annie treats disturbing sleep study participation as casual academic opportunity
jokes.explanation: Annie's academic obsession makes medical testing sound routine. The trailing off on the sound effect adds absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Another pillow fort? Kind of repeating yourselves, aren't you? That was a blanket fort. This will be a pillow fort. Way more difficult, way better.
jokes.setup: Jeff points out apparent repetition of fort-building
jokes.punchline: Troy and Abed's pedantic distinction between pillow and blanket forts
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's own tendency to repeat concepts, while establishing the characters' obsessive dedication to minute distinctions.

jokes.text: All difficult things are better, like carrying a disease or holding in a fart right now.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous examples that undermine the philosophical point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from disease to immediate bodily discomfort. The specificity of 'right now' sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: My brain starts getting weird this time of night. It's 10:00 a.m. You're welcome.
jokes.setup: Pierce excusing odd behavior due to late hour
jokes.punchline: It's morning, and his 'You're welcome' for the time correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - complete disconnect from reality delivered with unearned confidence.

jokes.text: I don't recall seeing Subway in my premenopausal, postfeminist experiential marketing class. Actually, I'm on the wait list for the premen/postfem/ex mark.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to sound academic about class names
jokes.punchline: Absurd abbreviation that sounds like medical condition
jokes.explanation: Satirizes both Greendale's ridiculous course offerings and Britta's pseudo-intellectual pretensions.

jokes.text: Gang, meet Greendale's newest student, Subway. Your name is Subway? Yep, using a groundbreaking but surprisingly legal process known as 'corpohumanization'
jokes.setup: Introduction of new student with corporate name
jokes.punchline: Fake-legal terminology for corporate personhood taken to dystopian extreme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate personhood laws. 'Corpohumanization' is perfect pseudo-legal terminology that sounds both official and horrifying.

jokes.text: I don't believe this. Come on, Subway, there is no way you're 5'10".
jokes.punchline: She focuses on his height instead of the dystopian implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Britta misses the forest for the trees in the most Britta way possible.

jokes.text: So you can vote? Actually, no, because technically I'm only a week old. Aww.
jokes.setup: Questions about Subway's civic rights as corporate person
jokes.punchline: Age restriction combined with group's sympathetic 'aww'
jokes.explanation: The group treating a corporate entity like a baby is perfectly absurd. The 'aww' reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Wow, did you know Greendale students are technically in the army reserves? Let's say a little prayer for peace.
jokes.punchline: Sudden revelation of military obligation and religious response
jokes.explanation: Completely random Greendale absurdity delivered with corporate earnestness. The prayer pivot is wonderfully weird.

jokes.text: Wait, since when do we have lockers? Uh, since registration day, 2009. Jeff, did you skip the Preorientation Freshman Welcome Seminar and Diversity Fire Circle?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous orientation activities he missed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - his self-absorption has real consequences. 'Diversity Fire Circle' is peak Greendale absurdity.

jokes.text: 'We're only allowed to bring one stuffed animal. I'm bringing Ruthie... But using her pouch to sneak in Nathan.'
jokes.punchline: Annie's elaborate stuffed animal smuggling plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent Annie moment - treating stuffed animals with spy movie seriousness

jokes.text: Halloween dance. Post-Halloween dance. Dance contest. Contest dance.
jokes.setup: Jeff reading old notices from his locker
jokes.punchline: Increasingly redundant and meaningless dance events
jokes.explanation: Satirizes school event naming conventions. The rhythm and redundancy create the humor.

jokes.text: Save Garrett? What's wrong with Garrett? Nothing now. We saved him.
jokes.setup: Jeff confused by campaign to save Garrett
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact confirmation of successful but mysterious rescue
jokes.explanation: Perfect mystery box humor - we never learn what Garrett needed saving from, but Annie's certainty sells it.

jokes.text: Wait, that's 'saved' Garrett? [shocked reaction to seeing Garrett]
jokes.setup: Jeff expecting to see rescued Garrett
jokes.punchline: Garrett's appearance suggests the 'saving' was questionable
jokes.explanation: Visual punchline to the 'saved Garrett' setup. Dark implication that their help made things worse.

jokes.text: Gesundheit. I didn't sneeze.
jokes.punchline: Jeff didn't sneeze, implying she responded to something else
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke about what sound prompted 'Gesundheit'. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Look at these idiots. Cattle, sheep... Other animals that travel in herds. I want to say snakes.
jokes.punchline: Her uncertainty about whether snakes travel in herds
jokes.explanation: Britta's pseudo-intellectual authority undermined by basic ignorance. The hesitant 'I want to say' is perfect.

jokes.text: I totally predicted this in my High School newspaper column 'Britta unfiltered.' Unfiltered. I get it. Get what?
jokes.setup: Britta proud of her clever column name
jokes.punchline: Annie doesn't get the water filter reference, deflating Britta's pride
jokes.explanation: Britta's self-satisfied moment deflated by Annie's genuine confusion. Perfect character dynamic.

jokes.text: You're a progressive woman of a more... liberated looseness.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to suggest Britta seduce Subway
jokes.punchline: Offensive euphemism delivered as compliment
jokes.explanation: Pierce's casual misogyny disguised as recruitment pitch. The euphemistic language makes it worse.

jokes.text: Microphones hidden in lipstick. Lipstick hidden in microphones. And the deadliest weapon of them all... The penis flytrap.
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual pun as 'deadliest weapon'
jokes.explanation: Crude pun that's more cringe than clever. The escalation structure works but the payoff is weak.

jokes.text: I am not a whore, and not that I've done the math, but if I were, I'd be the super classy kind that gets flown to Dubai to stay in an underwater hotel.
jokes.punchline: Specific, elaborate fantasy about high-end sex work
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific escalation that reveals Britta's psychology. The Dubai underwater hotel detail is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, silly me! This is a coupon for 20% off at bed bath & beyond. No need to bribe me, Troy. Actually...
jokes.setup: Troy showing permit for pillow fort
jokes.punchline: Dean reveals it's just a store coupon, then considers it as actual bribe
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Dean's corrupt pivot at the end. Shows both his confusion and moral flexibility.

jokes.text: But if we won, we might meet that dude with the curly fingernails. Shridhar Chillal? Not interested. I saw him on a talk show. He came off as pretentious.
jokes.setup: Troy excited about meeting Guinness record holders
jokes.punchline: Abed's dismissive celebrity gossip about fingernail record holder
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - Abed having opinions about the personality of an obscure record holder. The specificity and Abed's dismissiveness are perfect.

jokes.text: You and Abed have always reminded me of the inspector and his trusty constable Reggie. Cool! The inspector, of course, is smart, decisive, and Reggie is... Well, he's Reggie.
jokes.setup: Laybourne comparing Troy and Abed to TV characters
jokes.punchline: Dismissive non-description of Reggie that clearly applies to Troy
jokes.explanation: Subtle manipulation disguised as pop culture reference. The pause and dismissive 'Well, he's Reggie' is perfectly cutting.

jokes.text: Reggie is trained in zero-gravity martial arts and has a whistle. Yeah. But he never really gets to blow the whistle unless the inspector says it's okay.
jokes.punchline: Laybourne's response highlights the power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for Troy and Abed's dynamic. The whistle permission becomes increasingly pathetic.

jokes.text: Kim's no longer with us. She died two weeks ago. What? Sorry to drop that on you and run, but there's a rally for Garrett. But we saved him. Did we?
jokes.punchline: Garrett rally callback with ominous question about their success
jokes.explanation: Dark humor enhanced by callback structure. The 'Did we?' question adds ominous undertone to previous absurdist moment.

jokes.text: 'But we saved him.' 'Did we?'
jokes.punchline: Ominous suggestion that Garrett needs saving again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that deepens the mystery of Garrett's situation

jokes.text: What do you recommend? What's it to you, meat pusher? Sounds like you might be a vegetarian. You should try Subway's Veggie Delite.
jokes.setup: Britta's hostile approach to corporate espionage
jokes.punchline: Subway's professional response to obvious antagonism
jokes.explanation: Corporate programming overriding human response to hostility. Sets up the seduction subplot.

jokes.text: You should try reading Orwell's 1984. I have. It's a great book. It really awakened me in High School. I think kids should be forced to read it. Me too. Anyway, you're living it.
jokes.punchline: Subway's genuine agreement before Britta's blunt accusation
jokes.explanation: Ironic that the corporate person shows more genuine literary engagement than the activist. Britta's bluntness undercuts her subtlety.

jokes.text: Is that you, Subway? My eyesight ain't what it used to be. Eat these tater tots, Harry. For what it's worth, I think you might see more than all of us.
jokes.punchline: Subway's philosophical response to casual interaction
jokes.explanation: Subway's humanity bleeding through corporate programming. The philosophical response to tater tots is endearingly absurd.

jokes.text: Top-notch whoresmanship, Britta. Pierce. Sorry. Whoreswomanship. Forgot it was the '90s.
jokes.setup: Pierce's crude compliment about seduction skills
jokes.punchline: Fake-progressive 'correction' that's actually more offensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - attempting political correctness while being more offensive. The fake timeline reference adds absurdity.

jokes.text: 'Yep, that's me, the girl who's going to destroy the big sandwich company with the dreamy eyes.'
jokes.setup: Pierce and Shirley celebrating Britta's espionage success
jokes.punchline: Britta revealing she's attracted to the target
jokes.explanation: Good Britta self-awareness about her compromised position

jokes.text: Powerful passage, isn't it? 'She had become a physical necessity.' You circled this paragraph. I can't say that I did.
jokes.punchline: Subway's corporate-speak response to personal question
jokes.explanation: Corporate programming preventing normal human intimacy. The formal 'I can't say that I did' is perfectly robotic.

jokes.text: I want to run a nonprofit shelter for handicapped animals. Dogs with wheels for hind legs. Deaf hamsters. One-eyed cats? Well, they weren't on the top of my list, but sure.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific disabled animals with diplomatic response to Britta's addition
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdity of his specific charity vision. The polite consideration of one-eyed cats shows his diplomatic training.

jokes.text: Why do I always have to be your Reggie? What? I wanted to make a blanket fort, and you never even gave it a second thought. I'm not even your sidekick. I'm your underkick.
jokes.punchline: Troy's escalation from sidekick to 'underkick'
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to Laybourne's manipulation. 'Underkick' is a perfect Troy neologism that captures his frustration.

jokes.text: Have fun stacking pillows like a baby.
jokes.punchline: Troy's childish insult as he storms off
jokes.explanation: Troy's attempt at a cutting insult that's just childish. Fits the character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You think that's what an apology is? A spell you cast on another person to make them forgive you? Apologies are opportunities to admit your own mistakes.
jokes.punchline: Annie's insightful correction framed as magical thinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that exposes Jeff's narcissistic worldview. The 'spell' comparison is both funny and insightful.

jokes.text: But I was going to lie there. I was going to invest in IBM in 1952, but life is full of disappointments.
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison to missed financial opportunity from decades ago
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leonard moment - comparing trivial inconvenience to major life regret with identical resignation. The specificity sells it.

jokes.text: You never lived anywhere. You're a weapon designed for sex. You only think you lived in New York 'cause I implanted your memories.
jokes.punchline: Pierce's sci-fi megalomaniac fantasy about creating Britta
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation into sci-fi territory. 'Weapon designed for sex' is perfectly crude Pierce, and the memory implantation is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Did you just drink ink? Just plant this on Subway, and you'll never have to see him again. Until then, keep him interested. And for God's sakes, slap some life on those dead lips.
jokes.punchline: Casual drinking of ink followed by harsh beauty criticism
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of drinking ink enhanced by Pierce's casual cruelty. 'Dead lips' is particularly harsh and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: 'And for God's sakes, slap some life on those dead lips.'
jokes.punchline: Crude advice about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce insensitivity disguised as helpful advice

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll remember that when I'm being given a key to the secret guinness record holders' clubhouse, where I'll be hanging with those two fat guys on motorcycles and being fitted for a beard of bees.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about exclusive record holder community
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific fantasy that gets progressively more absurd. The 'secret clubhouse' concept is perfectly Troy, and 'beard of bees' is a great finale.

jokes.text: Is that what you want, Troy? For me to destroy my pillow fort so you could set your record? It is. Magnitude. Evacuate fort Abed, and prepare for self-destruct. Sir? Do it. We're done here. Pop, pop, Captain.
jokes.punchline: Military-style evacuation order ending with Magnitude's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from domestic dispute to military drama. Magnitude's participation and formal response is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Only the primal remains. To me, you are simply a physical necessity.
jokes.setup: Romantic scene between Britta and Subway
jokes.punchline: Britta quoting the 1984 passage they discussed earlier
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that shows Britta actually connecting with literature through romance. The callback structure is well-executed.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. No, this is wrong, Pierce. I just wanted to open a sandwich shop. I did not sign up for this.
jokes.punchline: Subway's corporate crisis over being recorded
jokes.explanation: Humanizes Subway through his distress. The simple desire to 'open a sandwich shop' is touchingly mundane.

jokes.text: Because you never remember who I am. First year, we hung out, like, ten times. And each time, you introduced yourself to me. That's why I wrote that note. And then today, it's like you still don't remember me. And... and you still assume from my name that I am a girl.
jokes.setup: Kim explaining why he wrote the angry note
jokes.punchline: Multiple layers of Jeff's thoughtlessness including gender assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that recontextualizes the entire subplot. Jeff's multiple layers of inconsideration are believable and well-crafted.

jokes.text: 16% of all people named Kim are men.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific statistic delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: The precision of the statistic is funny and suggests Kim has researched this frustrating aspect of his life.

jokes.text: I thought I was teaching you a lesson about all the girls you dominate and then ignore. Not to waste your energy on some weird, cloying, hypersensitive stalker with a girl's name. Hey! You are really mean. Put it in a letter, Jane Austen.
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cruel dismissal followed by literary insult
jokes.explanation: Jeff's cruelty proves Annie's point about his character. The Jane Austen insult adds literary pretension to his meanness.

jokes.text: This world is run by the unremarkables. Don't do what you always do, Abed. Don't corrupt the host to pacify the parasites.
jokes.punchline: Overwrought philosophical language about artistic integrity
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on artistic compromise. The pretentious language parodies art film dialogue while making a point about the show itself.

jokes.text: Now please turn your head while I crawl away. I'm going through some stuff right now. I'm very insecure.
jokes.punchline: Requesting to crawl away due to personal issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of mysterious mentor trope. The contrast between wisdom and vulnerability is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: That got unhealthy real quick. That... okay, that's... yeah, that's weird. That is well out of the mainstream. You know, I was raised in the... the Bay Area, but I... I'm a father now.
jokes.punchline: Progressive credentials failing him in face of sexual content
jokes.explanation: Corporate executive's discomfort escalating through attempted liberal credentials to parental concern. Good character work.

jokes.text: If someone could hand me my jacket. It's right over there on the coatrack next to the door. If somebody could just hand it to me, that would be great. I guess I'm confused. Why don't you just grab it on your way out the door... You know what? Now I'm not leaving.
jokes.setup: Executive trying to make dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Practical jacket logistics derailing his authority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended cringe sequence. The mundane jacket problem completely undermines corporate authority. The petulant 'Now I'm not leaving' is perfect.

jokes.text: 'If someone could hand me my jacket. It's right over there on the coatrack next to the door. If somebody could just hand it to me, that would be great.'
jokes.setup: Executive trying to make dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Inability to leave without help getting jacket
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of executive's discomfort undermining his authority

jokes.text: 'Now I'm just going to sit for a while and focus on how unacceptable today was.'
jokes.punchline: Deciding to process trauma in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing executive completely losing composure

jokes.text: Britta, you silly. It is me, Subway. What? I had a great time with you last night in the pillow fort, the one exception being the deviant sex act you initiated without my consent.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to non-consensual 'deviant' acts
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate replacement and consent. The casual delivery makes it more unsettling.

jokes.text: 'I had a great time with you last night in the pillow fort, the one exception being the deviant sex act you initiated without my consent.'
jokes.setup: New corporate Subway replacement meeting Britta
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning sexual assault in corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate replacement and consent issues

jokes.text: Watch it, Star-Burns. My name is Alex!
jokes.punchline: Star-Burns' ongoing identity crisis amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Running gag about his name preference. Good callback but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Soon Troy and Abed will be torn asunder, and an unencumbered Troy will turn to his destiny... The air conditioning repair school. You are a cold-blooded man, sir. Yes. How unfortunate for Troy and Abed.
jokes.punchline: 'Cold-blooded' pun about air conditioning and his character
jokes.explanation: Good pun that works on multiple levels - temperature and personality. Sets up the manipulation subplot nicely.
